name: eve-selfhost-spike
description: Spike #1030 — Eve self-hosted feasibility POC. All versions pinned EXACTLY (Eve is beta, ~41 releases/2wks; floating pins have broken before).

//pins.verified_by: npm install (114 pkgs, node v24.18.0) + `eve info` (compile ready, 0 diagnostics) + `eve dev` (boots on :2000). Tree inspected directly, not assumed.
//pins.eve: 0.19.0 — latest; requires node>=24. CORRECTION: eve's ONLY hard dependency is nitro@3.0.260610-beta (the HTTP server). There is NO @workflow/core in the tree — an earlier note claiming eve pulls @workflow/core@5.0.0-beta.26 was WRONG. Peer deps: ai ^7.0.0 (required) plus optional @opentelemetry/api, braintrust, just-bash, microsandbox.
//pins.ai: 7.0.11 — latest; satisfies eve peerDependency ai ^7.0.0. Note: eve@0.19.0's approval status values track AI SDK 7 ('approved'/'denied'/'user-approval').
//pins.zod: 4.4.3 — direct dep. WARNING: the beta tree ships THREE zod majors simultaneously — zod@4.4.3, zod@4.3.6 (x3), zod@4.1.11. They coexist today but this is the kind of drift that breaks on a floating pin; keep zod pinned exactly.
//pins.@ai-sdk/anthropic: 4.0.5 — latest. Only actually used if the agent's model is an SDK call `anthropic(...)`. With a STRING model id ('anthropic/claude-*') eve routes through the Vercel AI Gateway instead (needs VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN / AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY), so this pkg alone does NOT let a bare ANTHROPIC_API_KEY work — see findings.md AC1/AC2.
//pins.@workflow/world-postgres: 5.0.0-beta.20 (devDependency) — the OPTIONAL self-host durable backend (Postgres + graphile-worker). It is a SEPARATE concern from eve itself; installing it pulls @workflow/{world@5.0.0-beta.14, world-local@5.0.0-beta.22, errors@5.0.0-beta.8, utils@5.0.0-beta.4, serde@4.1.0}. NOTE: npm 'latest' tag for this pkg is 4.2.0 (STALE 4.x line); the 5.x BETA is the current runtime — exactly the floating-pin trap the issue warns about.
